project.name: rafeeq
project.description: Cross-platform Quran quiz and viewer app (Ionic React + Capacitor). Runs on Web, iOS, Android, and Electron.

nodes.name: VerseVisibilityContext.tsx
nodes.summary: React context providing cross-viewer verse selection and visibility (hide/show) state, with hidden verses persisted to localStorage and selection kept volatile in-memory only.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses useMemo to memoize the entire context value object, avoiding unnecessary consumer re-renders. Hidden state uses immutable Set copies to preserve referential equality checks.

nodes.name: VerseVisibilityProvider
nodes.summary: Context provider component managing verse selection and hidden sets, persisting the hidden set to localStorage on every change via useEffect.

nodes.name: useVerseVisibility
nodes.summary: Custom hook that exposes the VerseVisibilityCtx from context, throwing a descriptive error when called outside the provider.

nodes.name: useFeedbackBeep.ts
nodes.summary: Custom React hook that synthesizes short two-tone audio beeps for quiz answer feedback (correct/wrong) using the Web Audio API, with no external audio assets.
nodes.languageNotes: Handles both standard AudioContext and the legacy webkit-prefixed variant for broad browser compatibility. AudioContext is lazily initialized on first use.

nodes.name: useFeedbackBeep
nodes.summary: Returns a memoized beep callback that plays a correct (ascending G5â†’C6) or wrong (descending A4â†’E4) tone sequence via Web Audio oscillators.

nodes.name: useWakeLock.ts
nodes.summary: Custom React hook that acquires a Screen Wake Lock to prevent the device screen from dimming during quiz sessions, re-acquiring it when the page becomes visible again.
nodes.languageNotes: Follows the Screen Wake Lock spec requirement to re-acquire after visibilitychange events; silently ignores acquisition failures (e.g. battery saver mode).

nodes.name: useWakeLock
nodes.summary: Acquires and manages the Screen Wake Lock lifecycle, including releasing on unmount and re-acquiring when the page becomes visible.

nodes.name: arabic.util.ts
nodes.summary: Canonical Arabic text utility module providing number conversion to Eastern Arabic-Indic digits, diacritics stripping, and loose answer normalization used across all quiz and viewer surfaces.
nodes.languageNotes: Consolidates functions previously duplicated across multiple quiz files. Uses precise Unicode ranges for tashkeel and pause marks rather than broad character class matching.

nodes.name: toHindiNumbers
nodes.summary: Converts Western/ASCII digit characters to Eastern Arabic-Indic (Hindi) digit characters for RTL number display.

nodes.name: removeDiacritics
nodes.summary: Strips Arabic tashkeel diacritics and pause marks from text, used for fuzzy answer matching and search normalization.

nodes.name: normaliseAnswer
nodes.summary: Normalizes a string for loose quiz answer comparison by stripping diacritics, collapsing whitespace, and lowercasing.

nodes.name: QuizExitModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Shared confirmation modal component displayed when the user attempts to exit an active quiz, with localized cancel and confirm buttons.

nodes.name: AkmelAlAyahSetup.tsx
nodes.summary: Quiz configuration page for the Akmel Al-Ayah (verse completion) quiz, allowing scope selection by surah, page range, or juz along with question count, then persisting the config via Capacitor Preferences before navigating to the test page.

nodes.name: AkmelAlAyahSetup
nodes.summary: Main setup component managing scope selection (surah/page/juz), question count, and navigation to the quiz test page after saving config.

nodes.name: AkmelAlAyah.tsx
nodes.summary: Interactive test page for the Akmel Al-Ayah verse-completion quiz, supporting immersive Mushaf context mode, hint progression, answer submission with sound feedback, and a results summary screen.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements an immersive mode where the question card collapses to expose the full MushafContextViewer when the user opens context; wake lock keeps the screen on during active quizzing.

nodes.name: splitVerse
nodes.summary: Splits a verse text string at approximately the midpoint into displayed and hidden portions for quiz question generation.

nodes.name: buildQuestion
nodes.summary: Builds a QuizQuestion object from a raw verse, splitting it into displayed and hidden portions and populating all required fields.

nodes.name: AkmelAlAyah
nodes.summary: Main quiz test component orchestrating question loading, answer evaluation, hint progression, immersive context viewer toggling, and final score display.

nodes.name: AkmelAlNehayatSetup.tsx
nodes.summary: Quiz configuration page for the Akmel Al-Nehayat (verse ending completion) quiz, structurally parallel to AkmelAlAyahSetup with the same scope-selection and question-count UI.

nodes.name: AkmelAlNehayatSetup
nodes.summary: Setup component for the verse-ending quiz with surah/page/juz scope selection and configurable question count.

nodes.name: AkmelAlNehayat.tsx
nodes.summary: Interactive test page for the Akmel Al-Nehayat verse-ending completion quiz, splitting verses at the last waqf (stop) symbol rather than midpoint, otherwise sharing the same immersive quiz UX as AkmelAlAyah.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines a splitAtLastWaqf function that detects Unicode waqf/stop markers in the Quran text to determine the question split point, producing a more linguistically meaningful quiz boundary than a simple midpoint split.

nodes.name: splitAtLastWaqf
nodes.summary: Finds the last waqf (Quranic stop) symbol in a verse text and splits the string there, returning displayed and hidden portions with a found indicator.

nodes.name: MutashabihatSetup.tsx
nodes.summary: Quiz configuration page for the Mutashabihat (similar verses) quiz, supporting multi-select surah mode in addition to the standard page and juz scopes.

nodes.name: MutashabihatSetup
nodes.summary: Setup component for the similar-verses quiz with multi-select surah grid, page range, and juz scope options.

nodes.name: MutashabihatTest.tsx
nodes.summary: Interactive test page for the Mutashabihat quiz that presents groups of similar Quranic verses and challenges the user to distinguish them by completing the differing endings.
nodes.languageNotes: Unique among the quiz tests: supports a selectedVerseIdx to let the user switch the MushafContextViewer between the target verse and its similar sibling verses by tapping info-strip chips.

nodes.name: MutashabihatTest
nodes.summary: Main mutashabihat quiz component orchestrating group-based question generation, sibling verse navigation, answer checking via fuzzy matching, and immersive Mushaf context viewer.

nodes.name: mutashabihat.service.ts
nodes.summary: Service module for the Mutashabihat quiz providing algorithms to detect groups of Quran verses with shared opening phrases, filter them by scope, build question objects, and fuzzy-check user answers.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a 3-word sliding window to build a phrase map; groups with non-unique completions are discarded so every question has meaningfully different answers. Fuzzy answer checking requires 60% word overlap.

nodes.name: getAllMutashabihatGroups
nodes.summary: Scans an array of Verse objects to identify groups of verses sharing the same opening 3-word phrase but differing endings, returning structured MutashabihatGroup objects.

nodes.name: filterGroupsBySurahs
nodes.summary: Filters mutashabihat groups to only include groups whose verses belong to specified surahs, keeping groups with at least 2 matching verses.

nodes.name: filterGroupsByPages
nodes.summary: Filters mutashabihat groups to only include verses within a given mushaf page range, keeping groups with at least 2 matching verses.

nodes.name: filterGroupsByJuzs
nodes.summary: Filters mutashabihat groups to only include verses belonging to specified juz numbers, keeping groups with at least 2 matching verses.

nodes.name: buildMutashabihatQuestion
nodes.summary: Builds a MutashabihatQuestion object from a group by randomly selecting a target verse, computing the displayed/hidden split, and collecting sibling verses as distractors.

nodes.name: checkMutashabihatAnswer
nodes.summary: Fuzzy-checks a user's answer against the correct hidden portion using diacritic-stripped word overlap, returning true when at least 60% of words match.

nodes.name: InlineSelect.tsx
nodes.summary: Accessible custom select component rendering its dropdown list via a React portal with smart viewport-aware positioning (opens above or below the trigger) and mobile touch/scroll handling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses createPortal to render the dropdown list into document.body to avoid z-index and overflow clipping issues. Implements pointer-drag detection to distinguish taps from scroll gestures on touch devices.

nodes.name: InlineSelect
nodes.summary: Renders a trigger button and a portal-mounted ARIA listbox with smart up/down positioning, outside-tap dismissal, and scroll-aware close behavior.

nodes.name: MushafContextViewer.tsx
nodes.summary: Shared viewer component that renders a Mushaf page in context around a target verse, integrating hint-based word-reveal, page navigation, verse visibility state, and a big-text fallback mode detected via ResizeObserver.
nodes.languageNotes: Merges the global VerseVisibilityContext hidden set with a local revelation window so quiz hints only reveal up to the hinted word count while still respecting the user's manual hide state from the page viewer.

nodes.name: MushafContextViewer
nodes.summary: Main context viewer component managing page loading, partial verse revelation for hints, page navigation, next-verse progressive reveal, and width-based big-text mode.

nodes.name: verse.model.ts
nodes.summary: Canonical TypeScript type definitions for Quran verse data, quiz question shapes (standard and mutashabihat), and quiz configuration objects used across all quiz and viewer features.

nodes.name: ThemeContext.tsx
nodes.summary: Provides a React context for day/night theme toggling, persisting the selection to localStorage and applying it as a data-theme attribute on the document root.
nodes.languageNotes: Exports both ThemeProvider (context) and useTheme (consumer hook), following the standard React context pattern.

nodes.name: ThemeProvider
nodes.summary: React provider component that manages the current theme state, persists it to localStorage, and broadcasts changes via context.

nodes.name: useAudioPlayer.ts
nodes.summary: Custom React hook wrapping a single HTMLAudioElement that persists across renders, supporting keyed play/stop operations for per-ayah recitation and quiz sound feedback.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a ref to keep the Audio element alive across re-renders; lazily creates the element on first play() to avoid SSR issues.

nodes.name: useAudioPlayer
nodes.summary: Returns a play/stop interface with playing state and current key, toggling playback on duplicate key press and cleaning up the audio element on unmount.

nodes.name: useImmersiveMode.ts
nodes.summary: Custom React hook encapsulating tap-to-toggle UI chrome behavior for the Mushaf page, detecting double-taps on non-interactive targets while ignoring swipes and keyboard-open states.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately framework-light: returns refs-based helpers (registerTouchStart, maybeToggleOnTap) for integration with the caller's existing swipe gesture handlers.

nodes.name: useImmersiveMode
nodes.summary: Returns chrome visibility state and touch event helpers that toggle toolbar/nav visibility on double-tap, skipping interactive targets and suppressing toggles while a text input is focused.

nodes.name: user-api.client.ts
nodes.summary: HTTP client for the Quran Foundation User API covering streaks, activity days, user profile, notes, goals, and local bookmarks; handles OAuth token management with automatic refresh and broker fallback.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a three-attempt retry loop with exponential back-off; user token always takes precedence over the machine-to-machine broker token.

nodes.name: fetchStreaks
nodes.summary: Fetches the reading streak history for the signed-in user from the /v1/streaks endpoint.

nodes.name: recordActivityDay
nodes.summary: Posts a Quran reading activity record for the current day; silently skips if the user is not logged in.

nodes.name: fetchUserProfile
nodes.summary: Decodes user profile fields from the locally stored ID token without making any network request, avoiding CORS concerns.

nodes.name: fetchAllNotes
nodes.summary: Fetches up to 50 notes for the signed-in user; returns empty array if unauthenticated.

nodes.name: fetchTodayGoalPlan
nodes.summary: Retrieves today's reading goal plan including progress metrics; returns null if user is not authenticated.

nodes.name: AuthCallback.tsx
nodes.summary: OAuth2 redirect callback page that exchanges the authorization code for tokens and attempts to close its popup tab, displaying a success or error message to the user.
nodes.languageNotes: Relies on window.close() to dismiss the popup tab after successful token exchange, with a cross-tab localStorage storage event picked up by the main Account page.

nodes.name: AuthCallback
nodes.summary: React component that processes the OAuth callback URL, calls exchangeCodeForToken, then closes the tab or redirects to an error route.

nodes.name: oauth.service.ts
nodes.summary: Implements full OAuth 2.0 PKCE flow for web and native (Capacitor) platforms, covering sign-in, token exchange, refresh, sign-out, and cross-platform token storage.
nodes.languageNotes: Abstracts platform storage: uses localStorage on web and @capacitor/preferences on native, enabling the same code path on both Ionic targets.

nodes.name: signIn
nodes.summary: Generates a PKCE code verifier/challenge pair, stores the verifier, builds the OAuth authorization URL with required scopes, and opens it in a new tab.

nodes.name: exchangeCodeForToken
nodes.summary: Exchanges the authorization code for access, refresh, and ID tokens via the token broker, then stores them in platform-appropriate storage and clears the code verifier.

nodes.name: refreshAccessToken
nodes.summary: Silently refreshes the access token using the stored refresh token; throws NetworkError if the device is offline to prevent clearing valid stored tokens.

nodes.name: Account.tsx
nodes.summary: Main account page displaying user profile, reading streaks, goals, and notes with sign-in/sign-out controls; listens for cross-tab OAuth token storage events to update auth state without page reload.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses IonPage/IonContent from Ionic and useIonViewWillEnter to refresh data each time the route becomes visible.

nodes.name: Account
nodes.summary: Root React component for the account screen, managing auth state, streak/note/profile data loading, and rendering modal sheets for About, Feature Request, Terms, and Privacy content.

nodes.name: AccountModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable bottom-sheet modal component for the account page, supporting backdrop click and Escape key dismissal.

nodes.name: GoalsCard.tsx
nodes.summary: Collapsible card component for creating, editing, and deleting Quran reading goals, showing today's plan progress and a timeline of scheduled daily targets.

nodes.name: GoalsCard
nodes.summary: React component rendering a collapsible goals card with summary/form view states, CRUD interactions against the user API, and a timeline visualization after goal creation.

nodes.name: PageViewer.tsx
nodes.summary: Primary Mushaf reading screen orchestrating page navigation (swipe, arrows, surah picker), verse hiding/reveal quiz mode, per-verse recitation playback, activity day tracking, immersive mode, and a slide-up playback sheet.
nodes.languageNotes: At 1168 lines this is the most complex component in the project, integrating six custom hooks, four contexts, and ten service calls.

nodes.name: PageViewer
nodes.summary: The central viewer React component, managing page state, verse-hide quiz logic, audio playback synchronization, touch gesture handling, and activity reporting to the user API.

nodes.name: MushafPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders a single Madani Mushaf page using QPC V4 Tajweed glyph fonts, grouping words by line number with support for verse selection, hiding overlays, surah headers, and bismillah banners.
nodes.languageNotes: Loads page-specific glyph fonts asynchronously from an IDB cache; supports two layout modes (strict Madani lines vs. flex-wrapped big-text) and uses CSS data attributes for verse selection highlighting.

nodes.name: MushafPage
nodes.summary: Core Mushaf page renderer: loads fonts, computes surah header/bismillah slots, builds hidden-segment overlays from DOM measurements, and handles tap/long-press verse interactions.

nodes.name: NoteModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Bottom-sheet modal for viewing, adding, editing, and deleting per-verse notes; shows a login prompt if the user is unauthenticated.

nodes.name: NoteModal
nodes.summary: React component implementing a two-view (list/compose) note sheet for a given verse key, with CRUD operations delegated to the user API client.

nodes.name: VerseActionSheet.tsx
nodes.summary: Slide-up action sheet shown on verse long-press, providing translation, tafsir browsing, audio playback, bookmarking, and note-taking for the selected verse.

nodes.name: VerseActionSheet
nodes.summary: React component managing tabbed translation/tafsir display, audio playback toggle, bookmark state, and embedded NoteModal for a long-pressed verse.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Root application component that bootstraps the Quran app â€” seeds the text corpus, initializes Quran metadata, preloads page data and fonts, sets up OAuth deep-link handling via Capacitor, and wraps all routes in theme, language, playback, and verse-visibility context providers.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: React root component that initializes data services on mount, handles Capacitor deep-link OAuth callbacks, and renders the full IonApp routing tree wrapped in all context providers.

nodes.name: font.loader.ts
nodes.summary: Manages per-page Quranic font loading, caching via IndexedDB, and CSS font-face injection â€” including palette (colour) rules for word-by-word highlighting and a full preload pass across all 604 Quran pages.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses an in-flight Map to deduplicate concurrent font fetch requests for the same page, a pattern that avoids redundant network requests without a full promise cache library.

nodes.name: ensurePageFont
nodes.summary: Ensures the font for a given Quran page is loaded and injected into the document, using an in-flight deduplication map to prevent concurrent duplicate fetches.

nodes.name: loadOrFetchBismillahFont
nodes.summary: Loads the Bismillah font blob from IndexedDB cache or fetches it from the network and stores it for future use.

nodes.name: loadOrFetchFont
nodes.summary: Loads a per-page Quranic font blob from IndexedDB or fetches it from the network with cache-first IDB persistence.

nodes.name: injectPalettesForPage
nodes.summary: Injects light and dark CSS colour-palette rules for a page's font family to enable word-by-word verse highlighting.

nodes.name: setMonoPaletteColor
nodes.summary: Sets the monochrome palette colour used for uniform verse rendering across all loaded page fonts.

nodes.name: appendMonoRule
nodes.summary: Appends a CSS @font-palette-values rule for a single page font in the monochrome colour palette.

nodes.name: preloadAllPageFonts
nodes.summary: Sequentially preloads all 604 Quran page fonts in the background, yielding to the browser every 10 pages and reporting progress via an optional callback.

nodes.name: quran-data-provider.ts
nodes.summary: Unified Quran data access facade that tries the native SDK client first and falls back to the REST API client for every data operation â€” verses by page/juz, audio, chapters, juzs, hizbs, translations, tafsir, and recitations.
nodes.languageNotes: The trySdkOrFallback pattern separates SDK availability detection from individual fetch operations, making it easy to add new endpoints without repeating fallback logic.

nodes.name: trySdkOrFallback
nodes.summary: Attempts a data operation via the Quran SDK client and falls back to the REST API client on SDK unavailability or error, logging warnings on degraded paths.

nodes.name: fetchTranslationsByPage
nodes.summary: Fetches verse translations for a given page using the SDK/fallback pattern, with support for a specific translation edition ID.

nodes.name: fetchTafsirForAyah
nodes.summary: Fetches tafsir (Quranic commentary) for a specific verse using the SDK/fallback pattern, accepting surah number, verse number, and tafsir resource ID.

nodes.name: quran-sdk-client.ts
nodes.summary: Initialises the quran.com JS SDK client via a lazy Proxy, exposing an availability check and the singleton client instance used by the data provider as its primary source.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a JavaScript Proxy to lazily defer client creation and return rejected promises for unavailable methods, providing graceful degradation without throwing synchronous errors.

nodes.name: createClient
nodes.summary: Creates and returns the quran.com SDK client wrapped in a Proxy that returns rejected promises for missing methods, enabling safe optional-method calls.

nodes.name: isSdkAvailable
nodes.summary: Returns true if the quran.com SDK is available in the current environment by checking for the global QuranAPI object.

nodes.name: metadata.service.ts
nodes.summary: Central Quran structural metadata service that fetches and caches chapters, juzs, hizbs, and rub el-hizbs in IndexedDB, then exposes accessors for page-to-surah mapping, surah names, juz/hizb/rub start and end verses, and page estimation from verse keys.
nodes.languageNotes: The first 670 lines consist of large static data arrays (hardcoded page-to-surah mappings and JUZ_START_PAGES). The runtime logic begins at line 671 with initMetadata.

nodes.name: initMetadata
nodes.summary: Asynchronously initialises all Quran structural metadata caches (chapters, juzs, hizbs, rubs) by reading from IndexedDB first and falling back to the API, then builds the page-to-surah map.

nodes.name: getSuraForPage
nodes.summary: Returns the surah number for a given Quran page number using the in-memory page-to-surah map.

nodes.name: estimatePageForVerse
nodes.summary: Estimates the Mushaf page number for a given surah and ayah using the static page mapping data, returning a best-guess page when exact data is unavailable.

nodes.name: getJuzStart
nodes.summary: Returns the starting verse reference (surah:ayah) for a given juz number from the cached juz metadata.

nodes.name: getJuzEnd
nodes.summary: Returns the ending verse reference (surah:ayah) for a given juz number from the cached juz metadata.

nodes.name: getRubNumberForPage
nodes.summary: Determines the rub el-hizb number for a given Mushaf page by estimating verse positions and comparing against cached rub boundary data.

nodes.name: quran.service.ts
nodes.summary: Core Quran data service providing page-level verse retrieval with a two-layer cache (LRU in-memory + IndexedDB), full-corpus text search with Arabic normalisation, surah/juz/page-range verse queries, tafsir/translation/audio access, and a corpus seeding pipeline from a bundled JSON file.

nodes.name: getPage
nodes.summary: Fetches all verses for a given Quran page, checking the in-memory LRU cache, then IndexedDB, and finally the remote API before caching results at both layers.

nodes.name: getAllVerses
nodes.summary: Loads all 6236 Quranic verses across all 604 pages, triggering cache repair if any pages are missing from IndexedDB.

nodes.name: getSurahVersesList
nodes.summary: Returns all verses belonging to a specific surah by fetching pages within the surah's page range from the chapter metadata.

nodes.name: getJuzVerses
nodes.summary: Fetches all verses for one or more juz numbers via the API data provider, mapping raw API verse objects to the app's internal Verse model.

nodes.name: repairPagesCache
nodes.summary: Clears and rebuilds the IndexedDB pages cache by re-fetching all pages from the API, used as a recovery mechanism when cache corruption is detected.

nodes.name: preloadAllPages
nodes.summary: Sequentially preloads all 604 Quran pages into IndexedDB, tolerating individual page failures and reporting progress via an optional callback.

nodes.name: seedTextCorpus
nodes.summary: Seeds the full-text search corpus into the IndexedDB 'verses' store from the bundled /data/quran-text.json, versioned to avoid redundant re-seeding.

nodes.name: searchQuran
nodes.summary: Searches the Quran corpus with Arabic diacritic-normalised matching â€” supports verse-key lookup (e.g. '2:255'), full-text IDB search, and page-cache fallback, returning ranked results.

nodes.name: findVerseByKey
nodes.summary: Finds a specific verse by surah and ayah number by looking up the IDB verses store and then falling back to the page cache at the estimated page.

nodes.name: IDBService
nodes.summary: Promise-based IndexedDB wrapper managing the app's object stores (pages, fonts, meta, verses, bookmarks, tafsir, audio) with lazy database opening, CRUD operations, and a chunked bulk-put for large datasets.
nodes.languageNotes: Wraps the callback-based IDBRequest API in Promises manually (no idb library), handling upgrade migrations via onupgradeneeded with version-aware store creation.

nodes.name: idb.service.ts
nodes.summary: Defines and exports the IDBService class and its singleton instance 'idb', providing a typed Promise-based API over the browser's IndexedDB for all persistent storage in the app.

nodes.name: Search.tsx
nodes.summary: Quran search page component featuring a live-search input with debounced Arabic-normalised results, recent-searches history persisted in localStorage, and navigation to the full SearchResults page on submit.

nodes.name: Search
nodes.summary: Main search page React component managing live search state, debounced quranService calls, recent-searches display, result highlighting, and navigation to SearchResults.

nodes.name: SearchResults.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-page Quran search results component that executes a search from URL query params, renders an expandable verse list with copy/share actions, and navigates to the PageViewer on verse tap.

nodes.name: SearchResults
nodes.summary: React component rendering paginated Quran search results with expandable verse cards, share/copy actions, and navigation to the PageViewer.

nodes.name: index.tsx
nodes.summary: React application entry point that mounts the root App component into the DOM via ReactDOM.render.

nodes.name: LanguageContext.tsx
nodes.summary: Provides the app-wide language context (Arabic/English) using React Context, persisting the selected language to localStorage and synchronizing the document's lang/dir attributes.

nodes.name: LanguageProvider
nodes.summary: React context provider that manages the current language state, persists it to localStorage, and updates DOM attributes for RTL/LTR rendering.

nodes.name: useLang
nodes.summary: Custom hook that exposes the LanguageContext value (lang, setLang, toggle, t, isRTL) to any consumer component.

nodes.name: strings.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the complete bilingual string table (Arabic and English) for the entire app, covering all feature sections: home, azkar, quiz, mushaf, playback, settings, and more.
nodes.languageNotes: The AppStrings interface uses function types for dynamic strings (e.g. selectionCount, actionSheetTitle, times), which keeps interpolation type-safe.

nodes.name: mushaf.config.ts
nodes.summary: Declares the supported Mushaf rendering kinds (Tajweed, Uthmani, IndoPak, Imlaei) with their API word-field mappings and display labels used in Settings.

nodes.name: Azkar.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-featured Azkar page with category browsing, per-item counter tracking, swipe-to-reset gesture, haptic feedback (Capacitor + Web Vibration API fallback), and localStorage persistence.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the Capacitor Haptics plugin with a Web Vibration API fallback, handling the native/web platform split entirely in one component.

nodes.name: readAzkarFlags
nodes.summary: Reads vibration and sound flags from the shared settings localStorage key without subscribing to Settings state, enabling decoupled cross-feature preference reading.

nodes.name: haptic
nodes.summary: Dispatches haptic feedback using Capacitor Haptics on native platforms or the Web Vibration API on web, with three named feedback patterns: tick, complete, and reset.

nodes.name: loadCatState
nodes.summary: Loads per-category azkar counter and completion state from localStorage, returning safe defaults if nothing is stored.

nodes.name: Azkar
nodes.summary: Main Azkar page React component managing category selection, counter state, swipe-to-reset gesture handling, haptic feedback, and rendering category cards or detail view.

nodes.name: Home.tsx
nodes.summary: App home page displaying the Basmalah as the central focal element with the Rafeeq brand name and the shared BottomNavBar.

nodes.name: Home
nodes.summary: Home page React component that renders the full-screen Basmalah display with Rafeeq branding and the bottom navigation bar.

nodes.name: QuizList.tsx
nodes.summary: Quiz list page listing all available quiz types (Akmel Al-Ayah, Mutashabihat, Akmel Al-Nehayat) as tappable cards that navigate to their respective setup pages.

nodes.name: QuizList
nodes.summary: Quiz list React component rendering a grid of quiz entry cards with i18n-aware titles and descriptions, routing to quiz setup pages on tap.

nodes.name: Settings.tsx
nodes.summary: Comprehensive settings page managing all app-wide preferences (font size, mushaf type, reciter, Tajweed colors, quiz difficulty, azkar vibration, notifications) with debounced localStorage persistence and a Tajweed color-rule information modal.

nodes.name: loadSettings
nodes.summary: Loads and merges persisted settings from localStorage with defaults, migrating any retired mushaf kind values to the current default.

nodes.name: saveSettings
nodes.summary: Saves settings to localStorage and dispatches a custom 'rafiq-settings-changed' event so same-tab listeners (e.g. PageViewer) can react without requiring page navigation.

nodes.name: Settings
nodes.summary: Main Settings page component managing all app preferences with debounced auto-save, a Tajweed rules modal, and sections for display, language, Quran, quiz, azkar, and notifications.

nodes.name: SurahJuzSelection.tsx
nodes.summary: Surah/Juz/Hizb selection page for the Quran viewer, providing tabbed navigation over all 114 surahs, 30 juzs, and 60 hizbs (with interpolated rub quarter breakdowns) and navigating to the target page in PageViewer.
nodes.languageNotes: Derives rub (quarter-hizb) page ranges by interpolating within hizb boundaries because the underlying metadata service returns inaccurate rub data.

nodes.name: SurahJuzSelection
nodes.summary: React component for selecting a Quran navigation target (surah, juz, or hizb/rub), computing metadata-derived items via useMemo and navigating to the viewer on selection.

nodes.name: BottomNavBar.tsx
nodes.summary: Shared bottom navigation bar component used across all primary pages, rendering tabs for Home, Quran, Quiz, Azkar, Ahadith, Account, and Settings with active-state highlighting and coming-soon tab disabling.

nodes.name: BottomNavBar
nodes.summary: React component rendering the persistent bottom tab bar, supporting active tab highlighting, optional route override for the Quran tab, and a fixed-positioning mode.

nodes.name: azkarData.ts
nodes.summary: Static dataset of Islamic azkar (remembrances) organized by category (morning/evening, after prayer, etc.), sourced from Sahih Al-Azkar by Al-Albani, with text, source hadith references, and repeat counts.

nodes.name: PlaybackContext.tsx
nodes.summary: React context provider that wraps the playback queue hook and bridges it with the Android Auto / driving mode plugin, keeping the car UI synchronized with playback state and routing car control events (play, pause, next, prev, selectSurah, jumpToAya) back into the queue.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses stable refs (queueRef, setCurrentReciterIdRef) to prevent the Capacitor event listener from being torn down on every render â€” a common React pattern for long-lived native event bridges.

nodes.name: getQueuePageMarkers
nodes.summary: Computes one page/aya marker per Mushaf page that appears in a single-surah queue, used to expose page-jump buttons in the Android Auto car UI.

nodes.name: PlaybackProvider
nodes.summary: React component that instantiates the playback queue, registers the Android Auto content tree and car action listener, and exposes the PlaybackControls interface to all descendant components via PlaybackContext.

nodes.name: usePlaybackQueue.ts
nodes.summary: Core React hook that owns a single shared HTMLAudioElement and implements sequenced ayah-by-ayah Quran recitation playback with support for repeat-verse, repeat-range, playback rate, bismillah intro, background prefetching, Media Session API integration, and session recording.
nodes.languageNotes: Breaks a circular dependency between playIndex and advance by storing playIndex in a ref (playIndexRef) that advance reads â€” a common React hook pattern for mutually-recursive callbacks.

nodes.name: usePlaybackQueue
nodes.summary: Main hook export that manages the full lifecycle of a Quran audio playback session â€” initializing the audio element, coordinating verse sequencing with repeat logic, wiring Media Session actions, prefetching adjacent verses, and returning the complete PlaybackControls interface.

nodes.name: probeDuration
nodes.summary: Creates a temporary Audio element to read a blob URL's duration without full playback, used during background prefetch to build the total range duration estimate.

nodes.name: quran-api.client.ts
nodes.summary: HTTP client for the Quran Foundation Content API (api.quran.foundation/content/api/v4) that handles OAuth token acquisition via a token-broker, in-memory token caching with proactive refresh, retry logic with exponential backoff, and exposes typed endpoints for verses, audio, translations, tafsir, reciters, hizbs, and rub el-hizbs.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a single in-flight deduplication pattern (tokenInflight promise) to avoid parallel token requests and a 3-attempt retry loop that force-refreshes the token on 401/invalid_request before giving up.

nodes.name: getAccessToken
nodes.summary: Fetches a short-lived OAuth token from the token broker, caches it in module scope, and deduplicates concurrent requests by sharing a single in-flight promise.

nodes.name: apiFetch
nodes.summary: Generic authenticated fetch wrapper that injects the Bearer token, retries up to 3 times with exponential backoff, handles 401/400 by force-refreshing the token, and throws typed QuranApiError on failure.

nodes.name: fetchVersesByPage
nodes.summary: Fetches all verses for a given Mushaf page from the API and maps them to the internal PageVerseDTO shape, including word-level data for Uthmani text rendering.

nodes.name: fetchRecitations
nodes.summary: Fetches the list of available reciters from the API with an IDB-backed offline cache, returning cached data immediately when offline and persisting fresh results for future offline use.

nodes.name: audio-cache.service.ts
nodes.summary: IndexedDB-backed cache service for per-ayah recitation audio blobs, providing cache-check, download-and-store, blob URL creation, per-surah cache counts, and cache clearing operations used by the playback queue.
nodes.languageNotes: Stores audio as raw Blob objects in IDB keyed by 'reciter:sura:aya'; callers must revoke returned object URLs after use to avoid memory leaks.

nodes.name: getCachedOrDownload
nodes.summary: Primary entry point used by the playback queue: returns a blob URL from cache instantly, or downloads and caches the audio first, with a streaming URL fallback if caching fails.

nodes.name: downloadAndCache
nodes.summary: Downloads a verse's audio from the resolved streaming URL and persists it as an AudioRecord in IndexedDB, supporting AbortSignal for cancellation during background prefetch.

nodes.name: getCachedCountsPerSurah
nodes.summary: Scans only IDB key strings (no blob data) to return a map of surah number to cached verse count for a given reciter, used by the download management UI.

nodes.name: tafsir-cache.service.ts
nodes.summary: localStorage-backed cache for the list of available tafsir resources and for tracking which tafsir IDs the user has saved, with a custom event fired on changes so multiple UI components stay in sync.

nodes.name: driving-mode.plugin.ts
nodes.summary: Capacitor plugin wrapper for the native RafeeqAuto plugin that bridges the Quran app to Android Auto, defining the interface for pushing content trees, updating playback state, and receiving car action events, with a no-op web stub for non-native environments.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Capacitor's registerPlugin with a web stub to prevent runtime errors on web/Electron while the native layer is only active on Android/iOS.

nodes.name: recitation-history.service.ts
nodes.summary: localStorage service that records and retrieves recitation sessions (verse key, elapsed time, reciter, full queue) so users can resume a session exactly where they left off; upserts existing sessions by queue identity to avoid duplicates.

nodes.name: recordRecitationSession
nodes.summary: Saves a recitation session to localStorage, updating an existing session if one with the same queue is found (upsert by queue identity), or prepending a new session entry.

nodes.name: Bookmarks.tsx
nodes.summary: Ionic page component showing two tabs â€” saved verse bookmarks and past recitation sessions â€” with the ability to navigate to a verse, resume a recitation session via PlaybackContext, or delete individual entries; bilingual (Arabic/English) with RTL support.

nodes.name: Bookmarks
nodes.summary: Default-exported React component rendering the Bookmarks page with tabbed UI for saved verses and recitation history, wiring bookmark removal and session resume actions.

nodes.name: PlaybackSettings.tsx
nodes.summary: Ionic page/overlay component providing full playback configuration â€” verse range selection (with quick-select shortcuts for page, surah, juz, hizb, rub), reciter picker, speed control, repeat settings, and an offline download manager for surah audio; persists preferences to localStorage.

nodes.name: PlaybackSettings
nodes.summary: Main exported component for the playback settings screen, managing reciter selection, range configuration, speed and repeat preferences, and an offline download overlay for surah audio caches.

nodes.name: expandRange
nodes.summary: Expands a start/end VerseKey pair into an ordered flat array of all verses in between, walking across surah boundaries using chapter verse counts.

nodes.name: TafsirSettings.tsx
nodes.summary: Ionic page component for managing which tafsir commentaries are saved locally, listing downloaded tafsirs and available ones grouped by language, with add/remove actions and real-time sync via a custom window event.

nodes.name: TafsirSettings
nodes.summary: Default-exported React component that renders the tafsir settings page, fetching available commentaries from the API/cache and allowing the user to save or remove tafsir selections with animated save feedback.

nodes.name: MainActivity.kt
nodes.summary: Capacitor BridgeActivity entry point for the Android app, registering RafeeqAutoPlugin and configuring edge-to-edge transparent system bars on launch.
nodes.languageNotes: Extends Capacitor's BridgeActivity rather than AppCompatActivity, which is the standard pattern for Ionic/Capacitor Android apps.

nodes.name: MainActivity
nodes.summary: Android Activity that bootstraps the Capacitor bridge, installs the splash screen, registers RafeeqAutoPlugin, and sets up edge-to-edge UI rendering.

nodes.name: enableEdgeToEdge
nodes.summary: Configures the window to render behind system bars with transparent status and navigation bar colors, and forces light-on-dark appearance.

nodes.name: RafeeqAutoPlugin.kt
nodes.summary: Capacitor plugin bridging the JavaScript layer and the Android Auto media service, exposing JS-callable methods to push content and playback state to the car display, and forwarding car events back to JS.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses @CapacitorPlugin and @PluginMethod annotations for automatic JS binding. Holds a static singleton instance so RafeeqMediaService can call back into the JS layer without a direct reference.

nodes.name: RafeeqAutoPlugin
nodes.summary: Capacitor Plugin class exposing setContentTree and updatePlaybackState to JavaScript, and providing sendCarEvent to relay car hardware events back to the JS PlaybackContext via Capacitor listeners.

nodes.name: setContentTree
nodes.summary: Capacitor plugin method that receives reciter and surah lists from JavaScript and pushes them to RafeeqMediaService to populate the Android Auto content browser.

nodes.name: updatePlaybackState
nodes.summary: Capacitor plugin method that receives playback state from JavaScript (playing flag, track info, position, page markers) and forwards it to RafeeqMediaService to update the car display and notification.

nodes.name: sendCarEvent
nodes.summary: Notifies all JavaScript listeners of a car action event (play, pause, next, prev, stop, selectSurah, jumpToAya) by emitting a 'carAction' Capacitor event with action, reciter, surah, and aya payload.

nodes.name: RafeeqMediaService.kt
nodes.summary: Android Auto MediaBrowserService implementation that provides a two-level browsable content tree (reciters â†’ surahs), manages MediaSession playback state, handles hardware media button callbacks via SessionCallback, and displays a persistent media notification with page-navigation actions.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements MediaBrowserServiceCompat for Android Auto discovery. Uses a companion-object singleton so RafeeqAutoPlugin can call state-update methods directly. Page-nav custom actions use fixed slot indices to prevent Android Auto layout shifts.

nodes.name: RafeeqMediaService
nodes.summary: MediaBrowserServiceCompat subclass serving as the Android Auto entry point, managing a three-level content tree (root â†’ reciters â†’ surahs), a MediaSession with custom page-navigation actions, and a persistent playback notification.

nodes.name: onLoadChildren
nodes.summary: MediaBrowserServiceCompat callback that builds and returns the Android Auto content tree: root contains a single 'Choose Reciter' folder, reciters list contains scrollable reciter entries, and each reciter node expands to its surah list.

nodes.name: updateState
nodes.summary: Receives playback state pushed from JavaScript (playing flag, surah name, verse key, position, duration, page markers, current page) and updates the MediaSession PlaybackState (including prev/next-page custom actions), metadata, and the foreground notification.

nodes.name: buildNotification
nodes.summary: Builds the persistent media notification with a fixed 5-action layout (prev-page, prev-verse, play/pause, next-verse, next-page) where page-nav slots are always present but show no-op intents when unavailable, ensuring stable compact-view indices.

nodes.name: buildMediaSession
nodes.summary: Initializes the MediaSessionCompat, attaches a PendingIntent for launching MainActivity, wires the SessionCallback, activates the session, and sets the sessionToken for Android Auto discovery.

nodes.name: preload.ts
nodes.summary: Electron preload entry point that bootstraps the Capacitor runtime bridge by requiring electron-rt, making it the first script executed in the renderer's isolated context.

nodes.name: electron-plugins.js
nodes.summary: Empty plugin registry stub that exports a bare object as the slot for registered Electron Capacitor plugins; populated externally by the build or plugin installation process.

nodes.name: electron-rt.ts
nodes.summary: Core Electron Capacitor runtime that iterates registered plugins, maps each class method to an ipcRenderer.invoke call, wires EventEmitter-based plugins with deduplicating add/remove listener helpers, and exposes the complete API surface to the renderer world via contextBridge as CapacitorCustomPlatform.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld to safely cross the context isolation boundary, a best practice for Electron security; EventEmitter subclassing is detected at runtime via instanceof check.

nodes.name: addListener
nodes.summary: Registers an IPC event listener for a plugin class, deduplicating event subscriptions on the main-process side and returning a unique listener ID for later removal.

nodes.name: removeListener
nodes.summary: Removes a specific IPC event listener by ID and notifies the main process to stop forwarding the event type when no more listeners remain.

nodes.name: removeAllListeners
nodes.summary: Removes all registered IPC listeners for a plugin class, optionally filtered by event type, and signals the main process to stop forwarding each affected event.

nodes.name: ic_launcher_background.xml
nodes.summary: Android vector drawable defining the adaptive launcher icon background â€” a teal (#26A69A) filled square overlaid with a semi-transparent white grid pattern at 108x108dp, conforming to the Android adaptive icon spec.
nodes.languageNotes: Android adaptive icons require a 108dp canvas; the outer 18dp safe zone on each side is masked, so only the central 72dp is reliably visible. The grid lines are decorative and part of the Android Studio default template.

nodes.name: ic_notification.xml
nodes.summary: Minimal Android notification icon drawable that references the mipmap launcher foreground image; Android automatically tints it white on API 21+ to comply with the monochrome notification icon requirement.
nodes.languageNotes: Using a bitmap reference to @mipmap/ic_launcher_foreground satisfies Android's notification icon requirement that the drawable be single-color (the system applies its own tint).

nodes.name: ic_transparent.xml
nodes.summary: Minimal 1x1dp fully transparent shape drawable used as a placeholder or invisible icon â€” typically referenced in splash screen or adaptive icon configurations where a transparent layer is needed.

nodes.name: ic_launcher_background.xml
nodes.summary: Android values resource defining the `ic_launcher_background` color as white (#FFFFFF), used as the adaptive launcher icon background color for devices where the vector drawable approach is not applied.
nodes.languageNotes: This color resource name matches the drawable of the same name in res/drawable; Android resolves the correct resource type based on the referencing context.

Handles both day/night themes via data-theme selectors and LTR/RTL via dir attribute selectors throughout.

nodes.summary: CSS stylesheet for the quiz exit confirmation modal, defining backdrop overlay, dialog container, and action button pair layout.

nodes.name: QuizList.css
nodes.summary: CSS stylesheet for the quiz selection list page, styling quiz type cards with icons, descriptions, difficulty badges, and hover/active states.

nodes.name: AkmelAlAyahSetup.css
nodes.summary: CSS stylesheet for the Akmel Al-Ayah quiz setup page, defining sura/juz picker, difficulty selector, and start button layout with RTL Arabic text handling.

nodes.name: AkmelAlAyah.css
nodes.summary: CSS stylesheet for the Akmel Al-Ayah quiz test page, styling the verse prompt display, answer input field, hint/skip controls, score counter, and result feedback with RTL Arabic layout.

nodes.name: AkmelAlNehayatSetup.css
nodes.summary: CSS stylesheet for the Akmel Al-Nehayat quiz setup page, with sura/juz filtering controls, difficulty options, and start button layout sharing token-based RTL design.

nodes.name: AkmelAlNehayat.css
nodes.summary: CSS stylesheet for the Akmel Al-Nehayat (verse-ending completion) quiz test page, styling the truncated verse, multi-choice answer buttons, progress indicator, and score display.

nodes.name: MutashabihatSetup.css
nodes.summary: CSS stylesheet for the Mutashabihat (similar verses) quiz setup page, defining similar-verse-pair selection controls, difficulty picker, and start button with shared design tokens.

nodes.name: MutashabihatTest.css
nodes.summary: The most comprehensive quiz CSS file in the project, styling the Mutashabihat test page with side-by-side verse comparison, answer slot highlighting, keyboard/tap interaction states, timer, and animated result overlay.

nodes.name: Settings.css
nodes.summary: CSS stylesheet for the Settings feature page, defining section groups, toggle rows, font size preview controls, theme picker swatches, and language selector with design tokens for day/night theming.

nodes.name: TafsirSettings.css
nodes.summary: Stylesheet for the Tafsir Settings page, providing layout, card rows, action buttons, empty/loading/error states, and a spinner animation â€” all driven by design-token CSS custom properties.

nodes.name: PageViewer.css
nodes.summary: Comprehensive stylesheet for the Mushaf page-viewer feature, covering the top toolbar, navigation controls, page-flip animations, word highlighting, verse selection, tafsir side panel, and responsive media queries â€” the largest CSS file in the project.

nodes.name: BottomNavBar.css
nodes.summary: Styles the persistent bottom navigation bar shared across all primary pages, including tab items, active/hover states with per-category color theming, icon boxes, RTL-aware labels, a fixed-position variant, and a page-layout helper class.

nodes.name: InlineSelect.css
nodes.summary: Styles the InlineSelect dropdown component, covering the trigger button, dropdown panel, option items, and night-mode overrides using design token custom properties.

nodes.name: MushafContextViewer.css
nodes.summary: Stylesheet for the MushafContextViewer panel that displays a Mushaf page in a compact contextual view, including RTL header, gold-accented borders, navigation controls, and theme-aware background transitions.

nodes.name: MushafPage.css
nodes.summary: Core styles for the MushafPage glyph renderer, handling RTL Quran text layout with evenly-distributed lines mimicking printed Mushaf pages, big-text mode, Bismillah centering, word highlighting, and verse-end markers.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses justify-content: space-between to distribute Arabic text lines across the full page height, replicating the visual structure of a printed Mushaf. A dynamic font-size is computed by JavaScript and overrides the initial clamp() value.

nodes.name: NoteModal.css
nodes.summary: Styles the slide-up bottom-sheet modal for adding and editing verse notes, including backdrop blur, sheet animation with safe-area padding, handle drag indicator, header, textarea, tag chips, and action buttons with night-mode variants.

nodes.name: VerseActionSheet.css
nodes.summary: Comprehensive styles for the full-height verse action bottom sheet triggered on long-press, including backdrop, slide-up sheet animation, verse display header, tabbed navigation, translation/tafsir/audio panels, bookmark and note action buttons, and night-mode variants.

nodes.name: index.css
nodes.summary: Global CSS entry point that imports design tokens, applies a full-height reset on html/body/#root, sets the system font stack and font smoothing, and exposes safe-area-inset CSS variables for edge-to-edge display on Android 15+.

nodes.name: tokens.css
nodes.summary: Single source of truth for the Rafeeq design system â€” defines all CSS custom properties for brand palette, typography (Arabic and Latin font stacks), spacing, radii, transitions, layout, and both night (default) and day theme surface/text/gold/shadow tokens switchable via [data-theme] on the html element.
nodes.languageNotes: Imports Amiri and Scheherazade New from Google Fonts; uses [data-theme="night"] / [data-theme="day"] attribute selectors rather than prefers-color-scheme so theme switching is fully user-controlled at runtime.

nodes.name: types.d.ts
nodes.summary: Global TypeScript ambient declarations that allow CSS and SCSS modules to be imported as objects with string-keyed class name mappings, enabling type-safe CSS module usage across the project.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Cloudflare Worker acting as a secure OAuth2 and content-API token broker for the Quran Foundation, holding client credentials as Worker secrets and exposing endpoints for client-credentials token fetching (with in-memory caching) and authorization-code / refresh-token exchange â€” preventing secrets from being exposed to the client app.
nodes.languageNotes: Deployed as a Cloudflare Worker (edge runtime); uses module-level `cache` variable for in-memory token caching between requests on the same isolate instance. Supports both machine-to-machine client-credentials flow and user OAuth2 PKCE authorization-code/refresh flows.

nodes.name: handleOAuthToken
nodes.summary: Handles the /oauth2/token endpoint, exchanging an authorization code or refresh token for user access tokens by proxying the request to the Quran Foundation OAuth2 server with server-side client credentials.

layers.name: Feature UI Layer
layers.description: All Ionic React page components and feature-level UI screens: the Quran viewer, quiz flows, account, bookmarks, home, azkar, settings, tafsir, and their co-located CSS stylesheets

layers.name: Shared Components Layer
layers.description: Reusable Ionic React components shared across multiple features: the Mushaf page renderer, verse action sheet, bottom navigation bar, note modal, context viewer, and InlineSelect — each consumed by 3+ features

layers.name: Core State & Hooks Layer
layers.description: App-wide React contexts (theme, language, playback, verse visibility) and custom hooks that encapsulate audio playback, wake lock, immersive mode, and feedback beep behaviour

layers.name: Core Services Layer
layers.description: Backend-integration and data-access singletons: Quran data service, metadata service, API clients for Quran Foundation and user accounts, OAuth service, IndexedDB wrapper, audio cache, tafsir cache, recitation history, and the mutashabihat quiz algorithm

layers.name: Foundation Layer
layers.description: Lowest-level cross-cutting primitives: the canonical verse/quiz TypeScript model, Arabic text utilities, platform detection, i18n string table, static azkar data, global CSS reset, design tokens, and ambient type declarations

layers.name: Native Platform Layer
layers.description: Platform-specific runtime code: the Android Capacitor bridge, Android Auto media service and plugin, Electron main process, Electron preload/IPC bridge, and all their build configs, Gradle scripts, and Android XML resources

layers.name: Project Infrastructure Layer
layers.description: All project-level configuration, build tooling, CI/CD pipeline, PWA assets, the token-broker Cloudflare Worker, static web assets, build scripts, and developer documentation that support the cross-platform release pipeline

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: The README is the best starting point for understanding Rafeeq: a cross-platform Quran quiz and viewer that ships a single TypeScript codebase to Web, iOS, Android, and Electron via Capacitor. It describes all major features — the offline Mushaf viewer, three quiz modes, audio recitation, daily azkar, and OAuth-authenticated notes and bookmarks — along with the layered architecture that makes this possible. Reading it first gives you the mental map you need before tracing the code.

tour.title: Foundation: Types & Design Tokens
tour.description: Before exploring any feature, it helps to know the vocabulary the whole codebase shares. `verse.model.ts` defines the canonical TypeScript types for verse data, quiz question shapes, and quiz configuration — every service and component speaks in these terms. `tokens.css` is the design system's foundation, defining CSS custom properties for brand colors, typography, spacing, and day/night themes that every UI component inherits. Together these two files form the Foundation Layer: the primitives everything else builds on.
tour.languageLesson: CSS custom properties (variables) allow themes to be changed at runtime without JavaScript. Declaring `--color-brand: #...` on `:root` and switching a `data-theme` attribute is the idiomatic pattern for day/night mode in Ionic apps.

tour.title: Application Bootstrap
tour.description: `index.tsx` is the React DOM entry point — it mounts the root component into the DOM, keeping the bootstrap intentionally minimal. `App.tsx` is where the real startup work happens: it seeds the Quran corpus into IndexedDB on first launch, initializes structural metadata, handles OAuth deep-link callbacks from native platforms, and wraps every route in the global context providers. Understanding App.tsx reveals the initialization order of the entire application.
tour.languageLesson: Ionic React apps use `IonApp` and `IonReactRouter` as the outermost shell. Wrapping routes in context providers here — rather than inside each page — ensures every component in the tree can access theme, language, and playback state without prop drilling.

tour.title: Global State: Theme, Language & Verse Visibility
tour.description: App.tsx (Step 3) wraps the app in three contexts that govern the entire UI. `ThemeContext.tsx` toggles between day and night modes, persisting the choice to localStorage. `LanguageContext.tsx` switches the UI between Arabic and English and synchronises the document's `dir` attribute for RTL/LTR layout. `VerseVisibilityContext.tsx` tracks which verse is currently selected or highlighted across the viewer and quiz screens, enabling features like synchronized scrolling and action-sheet targeting. These three contexts form the Core State & Hooks Layer's user-facing half.
tour.languageLesson: React context with a custom hook pattern (e.g. `useTheme()`) is the idiomatic way to share global state without Redux. The context provides the value; a hook encapsulates `useContext` and the error boundary, making the API ergonomic for consumers.

tour.title: Offline Data: Corpus & Metadata
tour.description: Rafeeq is offline-first. `quran-text.json` ships all 6,236 verses inside the app bundle so the Mushaf viewer works with zero network access. `quran.service.ts` seeds that file into IndexedDB on first launch and then serves verse data through an LRU cache backed by IDB — fast reads without re-parsing the full JSON on every lookup. `metadata.service.ts` is the structural companion, providing chapter names, juz/hizb boundaries, and page-to-surah mappings that the viewer and quiz logic rely on for navigation and question generation.
tour.languageLesson: An LRU (Least Recently Used) cache keeps the most recently accessed verses in memory while evicting cold entries, bounding RAM usage on mobile. Backing it with IndexedDB means the first cache miss is an async IDB read rather than a full JSON parse, which is significantly faster on large corpora.

tour.title: Persistent Storage: IndexedDB Wrapper
tour.description: Every piece of persistent data in Rafeeq — verse cache, audio blobs, font files — flows through `idb.service.ts`. It wraps the browser's IndexedDB API in a clean Promise-based singleton, abstracting away the verbose IDBRequest/onsuccess callback pattern. Being a singleton means the database connection is opened once and shared, which prevents connection-limit errors on browsers that cap concurrent IDB connections.
tour.languageLesson: IndexedDB is the only browser storage that can hold binary data (Blobs) of arbitrary size, making it the right choice for caching audio files and font binaries. The singleton pattern ensures a single open transaction stream, avoiding the overhead of opening and closing the database repeatedly.

tour.title: Quran Data API Layer
tour.description: When online data is needed (translations, tafsir, enriched verse metadata), `quran-data-provider.ts` acts as a facade: it decides whether to use the official Quran Foundation SDK or fall back to raw REST calls, shielding the rest of the app from that decision. `quran-api.client.ts` is the HTTP client underneath — it handles token injection and request lifecycle for the Quran Foundation Content API. This two-level design means switching API providers only requires touching the provider, not every consumer.
tour.languageLesson: The Facade pattern (quran-data-provider) decouples consumers from implementation details. The client underneath handles cross-cutting concerns like auth headers and retries. This separation makes unit testing easier: you mock the provider in feature tests and only test the HTTP client against a real network in integration tests.

tour.title: Authentication & Token Broker
tour.description: Rafeeq uses OAuth 2.0 PKCE for user sign-in, and `oauth.service.ts` implements the full flow — authorization code exchange, token refresh, and native deep-link handling — for both web and Capacitor native targets. Because the app cannot safely embed a client secret, it delegates token exchange to `token-broker/src/index.ts`, a Cloudflare Worker that holds the secrets server-side and acts as a secure intermediary. This architecture means the client binary contains no credentials.
tour.languageLesson: OAuth 2.0 PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) was designed for public clients — native and single-page apps — that cannot keep a client secret. The code verifier/challenge pair proves the app that started the flow is the same one that completes it, preventing authorization code interception attacks.

tour.title: User Data API Client
tour.description: Once authenticated, `user-api.client.ts` provides all the user-specific API calls: reading and writing streaks, goals, notes, and bookmarks. It attaches the OAuth access token (managed by oauth.service.ts from Step 8) to every request and handles token refresh transparently. This clean separation between the Quran content API (Step 7) and the user data API reflects the fact that they hit different backends with different auth requirements.

tour.title: Audio Playback System
tour.description: Audio is one of Rafeeq's most complex subsystems. `PlaybackContext.tsx` is the React context that exposes playback controls app-wide and bridges to the Android Auto plugin so car displays can control recitation. Inside it, `usePlaybackQueue.ts` owns the actual HTML audio element — it manages the verse queue, handles repeat modes, prefetches the next ayah while the current one plays, and registers the MediaSession API so the OS lock screen and Bluetooth controls work. `audio-cache.service.ts` sits underneath, storing downloaded audio blobs in IndexedDB so they play instantly on repeat listens.
tour.languageLesson: The MediaSession API (navigator.mediaSession) lets a web app register metadata and action handlers (play, pause, seekBackward, nextTrack) that the OS exposes on the lock screen and in Bluetooth headset buttons — giving a PWA the same native feel as a dedicated music app.

tour.title: Mushaf Viewer: Page Rendering
tour.description: The Mushaf viewer is the centrepiece of Rafeeq. `PageViewer.tsx` is the screen component — it handles page navigation gestures, wires up the playback controls, and manages immersive (full-screen) mode. It delegates the actual rendering to `MushafPage.tsx`, a shared component that renders one Madani Mushaf page using QPC V4 Tajweed fonts. Font files are large and page-specific, so `font.loader.ts` downloads each page's font on demand, caches it in IndexedDB, and injects the `@font-face` CSS rule dynamically — ensuring the correct calligraphic rendering without bloating the initial bundle.
tour.languageLesson: Dynamic `@font-face` injection via JavaScript allows loading hundreds of font files on demand rather than upfront. The pattern: fetch the font binary, store it as a Blob in IndexedDB, create an Object URL or base64 data URI, then call `document.fonts.add(new FontFace(...))` to register it — no FOUC (flash of unstyled content) and no wasted bandwidth for pages the user never visits.

tour.title: Verse Interaction: Action Sheet
tour.description: Long-pressing any verse in the Mushaf opens `VerseActionSheet.tsx`, a shared slide-up sheet that aggregates everything a user can do with a single ayah: view a translation, open tafsir, play audio for that verse, bookmark it, or add a note. Because this component is used in both the viewer and quiz screens, it lives in the Shared Components Layer and consumes data from multiple services (playback, user API, metadata) rather than being owned by any single feature.

tour.title: Arabic Text Utilities
tour.description: `arabic.util.ts` is a small but essential utility that the quiz system depends on. It normalizes Arabic text for answer comparison by stripping diacritics (tashkeel), converting Eastern Arabic numerals to Western, and standardizing Unicode representations of letters that have visually similar variants. Without this normalization, a user's correct answer could be marked wrong due to a missing haraka or a different Unicode code point for the same letter.
tour.languageLesson: Arabic text normalization is non-trivial: the same letter can appear in multiple Unicode representations (e.g. hamza forms), diacritics are combining characters that must be stripped independently, and the range 0x0660-0x0669 covers Eastern Arabic-Indic numerals that look like digits but have different code points from ASCII 0-9.

tour.title: Quiz Engine
tour.description: Rafeeq has three quiz modes. `AkmelAlAyah.tsx` demonstrates the verse-completion mode — the user reads the start of an ayah and must complete it, testing memorization. The `mutashabihat` quiz is the most algorithmically sophisticated: `mutashabihat.service.ts` identifies groups of similar-beginning verses across the Quran (the verses that are most commonly confused by memorizers) and generates distractors specifically from those confusable siblings. Both quizzes consume the canonical verse types from Step 2 and the Arabic normalization utilities from Step 13.
tour.languageLesson: Mutashabihat (متشابهات) are Quranic verses with similar or identical opening words — a classical Islamic science. The algorithm finds these algorithmically by computing text similarity on normalized verse openings, then groups them so the quiz can present a verse and offer its mutashabihat siblings as wrong-answer distractors, targeting the hardest memorization mistakes.

tour.title: Cross-Platform Config & Native Bridge
tour.description: `capacitor.config.ts` is the root Capacitor configuration that ties the single web codebase to all native targets — it declares the app ID, plugin permissions, deep-link schemes for OAuth (Step 8), and platform-specific overrides for iOS, Android, and Electron. `RafeeqMediaService.kt` shows how the native side extends the app: this Android Kotlin class implements `MediaBrowserServiceCompat` to support Android Auto, exposing a content tree of reciters and surahs so the car's head unit can browse and play Rafeeq's recitations even when the phone screen is off.
tour.languageLesson: Capacitor's bridge works by compiling the web bundle into an Android/iOS WebView and injecting JavaScript-to-native bridges for each plugin. The `capacitor.config.ts` file controls plugin configuration (URL schemes, permissions, native overrides) without requiring changes to the platform-specific project files — keeping the cross-platform promise intact.
